explicit_text: I live in Ottawa with my partner and our three cats. Before the pandemic, rent in Ottawa was already rising, but during the pandemic, it became almost impossible to find affordable housing. Eventually, after being homeless for two months, we found a two-bedroom place in Little Italy, Ottawa, which felt like a fresh start. Ottawa has been both the backdrop and the setting for many of the challenges and changes in my life during these years.
implicit_text: I live in the capital city of Canada with my partner and our three cats. Before the pandemic, rent in this city was already rising, but during the pandemic, it became almost impossible to find affordable housing. Eventually, after being homeless for two months, we found a two-bedroom place in the neighborhood known for its Italian heritage, which felt like a fresh start. This city has been both the backdrop and the setting for many of the challenges and changes in my life during these years.

explicit_text: The COVID-19 pandemic hit just as my partner was going through a mental breakdown, which made everything much harder. It started around January 2020 when we first heard about it, but I didn't have a phone or internet until late 2020, so my understanding was limited at first. The pandemic worsened our housing situation, forced us to squat in a moldy place, and made accessing healthcare and food extremely difficult. It was a terrifying time with so many people around us struggling mentally and physically.
implicit_text: The global health crisis hit just as my partner was going through a mental breakdown, which made everything much harder. It started around January 2020 when we first heard about it, but I didn't have a phone or internet until late 2020, so my understanding was limited at first. The outbreak worsened our housing situation, forced us to squat in a moldy place, and made accessing healthcare and food extremely difficult. It was a terrifying time with so many people around us struggling mentally and physically.

explicit_text: During the pandemic, we tried to get to the food bank in Orleans, Ontario, but it was so far away and difficult to reach. Orleans felt almost like a ghost town with empty streets except for joggers during the lockdown. Our experience there was tough because the food bank was better than others, but we still struggled to get enough food. Orleans was part of the isolated and challenging environment we lived through at the pandemic's height.
implicit_text: During the pandemic, we tried to get to the food bank in a suburban community east of the capital, but it was so far away and difficult to reach. That area felt almost like a ghost town with empty streets except for joggers during the lockdown. Our experience there was tough because the food bank was better than others, but we still struggled to get enough food. That suburb was part of the isolated and challenging environment we lived through at the pandemic's height.

explicit_text: At one point during the pandemic, when food was scarce and we couldn't get to the food bank, we ended up eating out of the Giant Tiger dumpster for about a month. It was a desperate time, and Giant Tiger became an unexpected source of survival for us. That experience really highlighted how tough things got for us and many others during the crisis.
implicit_text: At one point during the pandemic, when food was scarce and we couldn't get to the food bank, we ended up eating out of the dumpster behind a popular Canadian discount store chain for about a month. It was a desperate time, and that store became an unexpected source of survival for us. That experience really highlighted how tough things got for us and many others during the crisis.

explicit_text: My art style has evolved over time, especially as my vision and arthritis worsened during the pandemic. I've been inspired by Frida Kahlo's later work, which became less precise as her health declined around my age. Like her, my art has become shakier and more about finding beauty in imperfection. Frida Kahlo's journey has helped me accept that art is something you are, not just something you do.
implicit_text: My art style has evolved over time, especially as my vision and arthritis worsened during the pandemic. I've been inspired by the later work of a famous Mexican painter known for her self-portraits and bold colors, whose art became less precise as her health declined around my age. Like her, my art has become shakier and more about finding beauty in imperfection. Her journey has helped me accept that art is something you are, not just something you do.

explicit_text: After being homeless for two months, we finally found a two-bedroom apartment in Little Italy, Ottawa, which was a huge relief. The rent was $1100 all-inclusive, which was affordable compared to what we faced before. Living in Little Italy brought us closer to the city's heartbeat and made life simpler and less complicated, even though the pandemic was still ongoing. It's been our home for about a year and a half now.
implicit_text: After being homeless for two months, we finally found a two-bedroom apartment in the neighborhood known for its Italian culture within the capital city, which was a huge relief. The rent was $1100 all-inclusive, which was affordable compared to what we faced before. Living in that area brought us closer to the city's heartbeat and made life simpler and less complicated, even though the pandemic was still ongoing. It's been our home for about a year and a half now.

explicit_text: My partner's health struggles during the pandemic reminded me of the Book of Job. He broke out in hives for months and suffered terribly, with boils that needed broken pottery to scrape at them. The whole ordeal felt biblical in its suffering and injustice, especially since he couldn't get proper medical help. The Book of Job captures that sense of enduring pain and hardship, which really resonated with what we went through.
